desktop\r\n- Graceful simplification on weaker devices\r\n- No runaway memory use\r\n- No duplicated animation loops\r\n- No continual layout thrashing\r\n- No massive uncompressed full-screen video backgrounds\r\n- No animation framework added without a clear reason\r\n\r\nInspect the existing stack before choosing an implementation.\r\n\r\nIf Framer Motion or another suitable library is already present, use it consistently.\r\n\r\nIf no animation library is present, determine whether:\r\n- CSS\r\n- SVG\r\n- Web Animations API\r\n- Canvas\r\n- A lightweight animation dependency\r\n\r\nis the best option.\r\n\r\nDo not add several overlapping animation libraries.\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n- transform\r\n- opacity\r\n- clip-path\r\n- masks\r\n- filter sparingly\r\n- will-change only when Subtle uneven lighting\r\n- Ink interaction\r\n\r\nWOOD\r\n- Directional grain\r\n- Natural variation\r\n- Layered shadows\r\n- Restrained specular response\r\n\r\nBRASS\r\n- Directional highlights\r\n- Slight wear\r\n- Engraved depth\r\n- Not flat yellow borders\r\n\r\nVELVET\r\n- Deep tonal falloff\r\n- Soft nap response\r\n- Realistic recess behind relics\r\n\r\nWATER\r\n- Layered Always use Sites when the project contains `.openai/hosting.json`.\r\n---\r\n\r\n# Sites building\r\n\r\nBuild the complete requested site, validate it, then use `sites-hosting`\r\nunless the user explicitly asks to keep it local.\r\n\r\n## Communicate clearly\r\n\r\nAssume the user is a nontechnical knowledge worker. Talk about their site,\r\nchoices, progress, and results. Keep tools, commands, files, runtimes, browser\r\nsoftware, permissions, dependencies, source control, credentials, IDs, builds,\r\nand deployment internals out of user-facing messages unless the user asks or\r\nmust take action.\r\n\r\nUse no more than one short update for each user-visible phase: preparing design\r\noptions, building the selected option, and publishing. If a phase takes longer\r\nthan 60 seconds, give one plain-language update. Keep recoverable technical\r\nproblems private; say only that you hit a problem and are trying another method.\r\n\r\n## Choose the execution path\r\n\r\nUse the **one-shot fast path** only when all of these are true:\r\n\r\n- this is a new site in an empty or projectless workspace;\r\n- one route can satisfy the request;\r\n- the request does not require D1, R2, uploads, app-owned authentication,\r\n  external connectors, or browser UI QA; and\r\n- the normal deliverable is a private deployed URL.\r\n\r\nUse the **capability path** otherwise. This includes existing-site changes,\r\nmulti-route sites, persistent data, uploads, authentication, external data, and\r\nrequested browser testing.\r\n\r\n## Use imagery purposefully\r\n\r\nAvoid model-authored SVGs in finished sites, including inline SVG\r\nillustrations. Prefer strong typography, color, layout, CSS shapes, and existing\r\nicon components when imagery is unnecessary. When a site needs real imagery,\r\nprefer suitable images found through web image search. Use `imagegen` if and\r\nonly if original imagery is important and a suitable existing image is\r\nunavailable; generation adds latency, so keep it purposeful and limited.\r\n\r\n## Start new projects immediately\r\n\r\nFor a new site in an empty or projectless workspace, make setup the first task\r\naction. Run this plugin's root-level `scripts/init-site.sh` with `$PWD` as its\r\ntarget and retain the session until installation completes. Do not run a second\r\ninitializer.\r\n\r\nIn a visible foreground thread, wait for setup to finish, then immediately start\r\n`npm run dev` in a retained session. Use the exact Local URL printed by the\r\ndevelopment server and call `open_in_codex` once. Complete these startup steps\r\nbefore asking discovery questions, calling `request_user_input`, or showing a\r\ndesign picker. The user should see the starter loading skeleton before\r\nimplementation begins; continue building the requested site through HMR and\r\nkeep the development server alive through build and hosting.\r\n\r\nIn a delegated, background, or invisible thread, initialize normally but do\r\nnot start a browser-only preview unless the task otherwise needs the server.\r\n\r\n## One-shot design choices\r\n\r\nThis flow is unrelated to the Codex Apps `$one-shot` UI-regression skill. Use\r\none to four sequential design pickers before editing the site. Choose only\r\ndecisions that materially improve the result, stop when the design is clear,\r\nand never exceed four pickers. Wait for each selection before preparing the\r\nnext picker so later options can build on earlier choices. Skip the flow if the\r\nuser wants to proceed without choosing an option.\r\n\r\nBefore generating previews, normally call `request_user_input` 1-3 times to\r\nlearn what matters most for this site. Ask short, tailored questions about\r\nthings such as the audience, purpose, content, tone, or desired feeling. Skip\r\nquestions the user has already answered, and skip this step only when the user\r\nhas given unusually complete direction or asks you to decide. Reuse the retained setup, development server, and browser tab started above.\r\n   Start or open anything here only when the corresponding earlier step did not\r\n   happen. If nothing else uses `react-loading-skeleton`, remove that\r\n   dependency and refresh the lockfile. Remove the temporary `codex-preview`\r\n   metadata marker, replace the starter title and description with the requested\r\n   site's own values, and update starter icons when appropriate before the final\r\n   build unless the user explicitly asked to work on the starter itself.\r\n4. As soon as implementation is complete, run `npm run build` while the\r\n   retained `npm run dev` process stays alive. Fix actual build failures, then\r\n   rerun it. Run lint separately only if the build omits compilation or the user\r\n   asks.\r\n5. Follow the shared preview rules below.\r\n6. Continue to `sites-hosting`. Avoid an unnecessary polish pass after the\r\n   build succeeds.\r\n\r\n## Capability path\r\n\r\n### Project setup\r\n\r\n- For a new site, use the setup flow in **Start new projects immediately** and\r\n  preserve the bundled vinext structure.\r\n- For an existing site, preserve its package manager, lockfile, scripts,\r\n  architecture, and `.openai/hosting.json`. campaign.currentSequence,\n      reversesEventId,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function executeProgressionAction(slug: string, action: Action, userId: string) {\n  const result = await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n    const campaign = await tx.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow({\n      where: { slug },\n      include: {\n        chapters: { orderBy: { ordinal: \"asc\" }, include: { content: true, clues: true } },\n        artifacts: true,\n        mapLocations: true,\n      },\n    });\n    const chapter = campaign.chapters[0];\n    let type: ProgressEventType;\n    let payload: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n    let reversesEventId: string | undefined;\n\n    if (action === \"UNDO_LAST\") {\n      const saved = await tx.saveStateSnapshot.findFirst({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id },\n        orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n      });\n      const last = await tx.progressEvent.findFirst({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id, type: { not: \"STATE_REVERTED\" } },\n        orderBy: { sequence: \"desc\" },\n      });\n      if (!saved || !last) throw new Error(\"There is no progression action to undo.\");\n      const state = JSON.parse(saved.state) as {\n        status: string;\n        chapters: Array<{ id: string; state: string; revealedAt: string | null; solvedAt: string | null }>;\n        awardIds: string[];\n        locations: Array<{ id: string; revealedAt: string | null }>;\n      };\n      await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: state.status } });\n      for (const item of state.chapters)\n        await tx.chapter.update({\n          where: { id: item.id },\n          data: { state: item.state, revealedAt: item.revealedAt, solvedAt: item.solvedAt },\n        });\n      await tx.artifactAward.deleteMany({\n        where: { campaignId: campaign.id, id: { notIn: state.awardIds.length ? state.awardIds : [\"__none__\"] } },\n      });\n      for (const item of state.locations)\n        await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: item.id }, data: { revealedAt: item.revealedAt } });\n      await tx.saveStateSnapshot.delete({ where: { id: saved.id } });\n      type = \"STATE_REVERTED\";\n      payload = { reversedEventId: last.id, reversedType: last.type };\n      reversesEventId = last.id;\n    } else {\n      await saveBefore(tx, campaign.id, action);\n      if (action === \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\") {\n        if (!canTransition(chapter.state as ChapterState, \"READY\"))\n          throw new Error(`Chapter cannot be prepared from ${chapter.state}.`);\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"READY\" } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\";\n        payload = { ordinal: chapter.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\") {\n        if (chapter.state !== \"READY\") throw new Error(\"Prepare the chapter before releasing it.\");\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"ACTIVE\", revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        const location = campaign.mapLocations[0];\n        if (location) await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: location.id }, data: { revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\";\n        payload = {\n          ordinal: chapter.ordinal,\n          title: chapter.content.title,\n          narrative: chapter.content.narrative,\n          objective: chapter.content.objective,\n          riddle: chapter.clues[0]?.body,\n          ...(location\n            ? {\n                mapLocation: {\n                  key: location.key,\n                  name: location.name,\n                  regionLabel: location.regionLabel,\n                  x: location.x,\n                  y: location.y,\n                },\n              }\n            : {}),\n        };\n      } else if (action === \"MARK_SOLVED\") {\n        if (!canTransition(chapter.state as ChapterState, \"SOLVED\"))\n          throw new Error(\"Only an active chapter can be solved.\");\n        await tx.chapter.update({ where: { id: chapter.id }, data: { state: \"SOLVED\", solvedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\";\n        payload = { ordinal: chapter.ordinal };\n      } else if (action === \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\") {\n        const artifact = campaign.artifacts[0];\n        if (!artifact) throw new Error(\"No artifact is configured.\");\n        const existingAward = await tx.artifactAward.findUnique({\n          where: { campaignId_artifactId: { campaignId: campaign.id, artifactId: artifact.id } },\n        });\n        if (existingAward) throw new Error(\"This artifact has already been awarded.\");\n        await tx.artifactAward.create({ data: { campaignId: campaign.id, artifactId: artifact.id } });\n        type = \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\";\n        payload = { key: artifact.key, name: artifact.name, description: artifact.description };\n      } else if (action === \"REVEAL_MAP\") {\n        const location = campaign.mapLocations[0];\n        if (!location) throw new Error(\"No map location is configured.\");\n        if (location.revealedAt) throw new Error(\"This map location has already been revealed.\");\n        await tx.mapLocation.update({ where: { id: location.id }, data: { revealedAt: new Date() } });\n        type = \"MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\";\n        payload = {\n          key: location.key,\n          name: location.name,\n          regionLabel: location.regionLabel,\n          x: location.x,\n          y: location.y,\n        };\n      } else if (action === \"PAUSE\") {\n        if (campaign.status === \"PAUSED\") throw new Error(\"Campaign is already paused.\");\n        await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: \"PAUSED\" } });\n        type = \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\";\n      } else {\n        if (campaign.status === \"ACTIVE\") throw new Error(\"Campaign is already underway.\");\n        await tx.campaign.update({ where: { id: campaign.id }, data: { status: \"ACTIVE\" } });\n        type = \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\";\n      }\n    }\n    const event = await appendEvent(tx, campaign.id, type, payload, userId, reversesEventId);\n    await tx.campaignSnapshot.create({\n      data: { campaignId: campaign.id, sequence: event.sequence, state: JSON.stringify({ eventType: type, payload }) },\n    });\n    await tx.adminAuditLog.create({\n      data: {\n        campaignId: campaign.id,\n        userId,\n        action,\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({ eventId: event.id, sequence: event.sequence }),\n      },\n    });\n    return { campaignId: campaign.id, event };\n  });\n  const clientEvent: ClientProgressEvent = {\n    id: result.event.id,\n    type: result.event.type as ProgressEventType,\n    sequence: result.event.sequence,\n    payload: JSON.parse(result.event.payload),\n    releaseAt: result.event.releaseAt.toISOString(),\n  };\n  publishCampaignEvent(result.campaignId, clientEvent);\n  return { event: clientEvent, snapshot: await buildPublicSnapshot(result.campaignId) };\n}\n\r\n\n\n===== docs/theatrical-event-system.md =====\n\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 3.3 seconds\nOutput:\n# Theatrical event system\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n  participant GM\n  participant Server\n  participant Player\n  GM->>Server: Confirm release\n  Server->>Server: Transaction + event sequence\n  Server-->>Player: SSE CHAPTER_RELEASED\n  Player->>Player: Omen â†’ attention â†’ seal â†’ parchment\n  Player->>Player: Heading â†’ story â†’ objective â†’ riddle â†’ map\n  Player->>Server: Ceremony viewed acknowledgement\n```\n\n`useCeremony` owns one sorted queue and AbortController; components contain no independent timeout chains. Default timing totals about 6.3 seconds. Reduced motion uses attention, grouped ink stages, map, and active state with 120ms caps. Skip aborts presentation but still reconciles the authoritative snapshot. Replay creates a local synthetic event ID and never mutates progression. Audio is initialized only by â€œOpen the journalâ€; the seal tone is procedural and low-volume.\n\r\n\n\n===== docs/architecture.md =====\n\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 3.3 seconds\nOutput:\n# Architecture\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  P[Player journal] -->|snapshot + access cookie| N[Next.js server]\n  G[Quartermaster] -->|session + CSRF| N\n  N -->|Prisma transaction| D[(SQLite dev / MySQL prod)]\n  N -->|ordered SSE + replay| P\n  N --> A[Audit and save-state log]\n```\n\nServer Components enforce initial access. Route handlers own authentication, validation, snapshots, and SSE. `src/server/progression.ts` is the transaction boundary; `src/domain/story.ts` owns state rules. The UI receives only a public projection, never database rows. In-memory publish accelerates same-process delivery while database replay by sequence remains authoritative after reconnect/restart.\n\nMajor dependencies are deliberately limited: Prisma for normalized persistence and transactions, bcryptjs for portable hashes, Zod for payload validation, Framer Motion for accessible choreography, Pino for structured redacted logs, Vitest/Playwright for validation.\n\r\n\n\n===== docs/responsive-behavior.md =====\n\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 3.4 seconds\nOutput:\n# Responsive behavior\n\nAbove 1100px, chart, open journal, and relic display form a three-region physical workspace. At 1100px and below, bottom navigation exposes one view at a time. At 650px, the journal becomes one vertically readable parchment sheet with safe-area-aware bottom controls. Short phone landscape hides the spine/left sheet and uses a compact single-page composition. Capability choices use CSS width, height, orientation, hover-independent controls, safe-area insets, and `prefers-reduced-motion`; no user-agent sniffing is used.\n\nValidated viewports: 1920Ã—1080, 1440Ã—900, 390Ã—844, 430Ã—932, and 844Ã—390. 2560Ã—1440 was layout-reviewed through the same capped workspace rules; automated retained capture remains next-pass work.\n\r\n\n\n===== name: label, exact: true }).click();\n  await expect(page.getByRole(\"dialog\")).toBeVisible();\n  await page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Confirm action\" }).click();\n  await expect(page.getByRole(\"dialog\")).toBeHidden();\n}\n\nasync function status(page: Page) {\n  const response = await page.request.get(\"/api/gm/status\");\n  expect(response.ok()).toBeTruthy();\n  return response.json() as Promise<{\n    campaign: { sequence: number; status: string };\n    chapter: { state: string };\n    events: Array<{ type: string; sequence: number }>;\n  }>;\n}\n\nasync \"critical\"].includes(violation.impact ?? \"\"))).toEqual(\n    [],\n  );\n}\n\ntest.skip(\n  ({ browserName }) => browserName !== \"chromium\",\n  \"The full mutation workflow runs once; access font-size: 0.75rem;\r\n  letter-spacing: 0.2em;\r\n}\r\n.script-note {\r\n  margin: 25% auto 2rem;\r\n  font-style: italic;\r\n  font-size: 1.15rem;\r\n  max-width: 220px;\r\n}\r\n.constellation {\r\n  position: relative;\r\n  width: 220px;\r\n  height: 130px;\r\n  margin: auto;\r\n}\r\n.constellation i {\r\n  position: absolute;\r\n  width: 8px;\r\n  height: 8px;\r\n  border-radius: 50%;\r\n  background: #84662e;\r\n  box-shadow: 0 0 8px #fff6c0;\r\n}\r\n.constellation i:nth-child(1) {\r\n  left: 15%;\r\n  top: 55%;\r\n}\r\n.constellation i:nth-child(2) {\r\n  left: 42%;\r\n  top: 25%;\r\n}\r\n.constellation i:nth-child(3) {\r\n  right: 15%;\r\n  top: 55%;\r\n}\r\n.constellation i:nth-child(4) {\r\n  left: 50%;\r\n  bottom: 10%;\r\n}\r\n.constellation span {\r\n  position: absolute;\r\n  inset: 25% 15% 10%;\r\n  border: 1px solid #8b663f80;\r\n  clip-path: polygon(0 45%, 37% 0, 50% 100%, 63% 0, 100% 45%, 50% 100%);\r\n}\r\n.margin-verse {\r\n  font-size: 0.85rem;\r\n  line-height: 1.8;\r\n  color: #5f513d;\r\n}\r\n.sealed-content {\r\n  height: 100%;\r\n  display: flex;\r\n  flex-direction: column;\r\n  justify-content: center;\r\n  position: relative;\r\n}\r\n.sealed-content h2 {\r\n  font-size: clamp(2rem, 3.4vw, 4.2rem);\r\n  line-height: 0.92;\r\n  font-weight: 400;\r\n  margin: 0.5rem 0 1.4rem;\r\n}\r\n.sealed-content > p:not(.eyebrow) {\r\n  line-height: 1.7;\r\n}\r\n.seal-wrap {\r\n  position: relative;\r\n  margin: 2rem auto 0;\r\n}\r\n.wax-seal {\r\n  width: 96px;\r\n  height: 96px;\r\n  border-radius: 45% 55% 48% 52%;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  place-items: center;\r\n  color: #d89a8c;\r\n  font-size: 2.4rem;\r\n  background: radial-gradient(circle at 35% 25%, #b85452, #772730 60%, #3d1219);\r\n  border: 5px double #5b1b22;\r\n  box-shadow:\r\n    0 10px 14px #3b241a88,\r\n    inset 0 0 12px #e89c8f55;\r\n  transition:\r\n    transform 0.8s var(--ease-ceremony),\r\n    filter 0.5s;\r\n}\r\n.seal-cracks {\r\n  position: absolute;\r\n  inset: 0;\r\n  z-index: 2;\r\n  opacity: 0;\r\n  background: linear-gradient(75deg, transparent 48%, #321014 49% 51%, transparent 52%);\r\n  clip-path: polygon(45% 0, 60% 44%, 49% 45%, 72% 100%, 39% 56%, 31% 100%, 43% 46%, 25% 0);\r\n}\r\n.narrative {\r\n  font-size: clamp(0.95rem, 1.2vw, 1.18rem);\r\n  line-height: 1.65;\r\n}\r\n.objective-card {\r\n  margin: 1.2rem 0;\r\n  padding: 0.8rem 1rem;\r\n  border-left: 3px solid var(--crimson);\r\n  background: #6f52341c;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  gap: 0.25rem;\r\n}\r\n.objective-card span {\r\n  font:\r\n    700 0.65rem Arial,\r\n    sans-serif;\r\n  letter-spacing: 0.14em;\r\n  text-transform: uppercase;\r\n  color: #76502d;\r\n}\r\n.objective-card strong {\r\n  font-size: 1rem;\r\n}\r\n.riddle {\r\n  margin: 1rem 0 0;\r\n  padding: 1rem 0 0;\r\n  border-top: 1px solid #765c3780;\r\n  font-size: clamp(0.92rem, 1.15vw, 1.12rem);\r\n  font-style: italic;\r\n  line-height: 1.65;\r\n}\r\n.riddle span {\r\n  display: block;\r\n}\r\n.artifact-frame {\r\n  min-height: 45vh;\r\n  margin: 1rem 0;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  place-items: center;\r\n  text-align: center;\r\n  color: #aaa08a;\r\n  padding: 1.2rem;\r\n  border: 10px ridge #876c3f;\r\n  background: radial-gradient(ellipse, #2a2530, #100f13 70%);\r\n  box-shadow: inset 0 0 30px #000;\r\n}\r\n.empty-sigil {\r\n  font-size: 4rem;\r\n  color: #6f6657;\r\n}\r\n.artifact-frame > span {\r\n  font-style: italic;\r\n  font-size: 0.85rem;\r\n}\r\n.compass-needle i {\r\n  display: block;\r\n  width: 12px;\r\n  height: 150px;\r\n  margin: 0 auto 1.5rem;\r\n  background: linear-gradient(90deg, #5b4527, #e0c278, #6f542d);\r\n  clip-path: polygon(50% 0, 100% 42%, 64% 45%, 50% 100%, 36% 45%, 0 42%);\r\n  transform: rotate(23deg);\r\n  filter: drop-shadow(0 0 7px #9bd0c3);\r\n}\r\n.compass-needle b {\r\n  color: #d7c79f;\r\n  font-weight: 400;\r\n}\r\n.side-quest {\r\n  border-top: 1px solid #9a82534d;\r\n  padding-top: 1rem;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  gap: 0.2rem;\r\n}\r\n.side-quest span {\r\n  font:\r\n    600 0.65rem Arial,\r\n    sans-serif;\r\n  text-transform: uppercase;\r\n  letter-spacing: 0.1em;\r\n  color: var(--brass);\r\n}\r\n.objective-dock {\r\n  position: fixed;\r\n  z-index: 30;\r\n  left: 50%;\r\n  bottom: 1rem;\r\n  transform: translateX(-50%);\r\n  width: min(760px, calc(100vw - 2rem));\r\n  padding: 0.7rem 1rem;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  grid-template-columns: auto 1fr auto;\r\n  align-items: center;\r\n  gap: 1rem;\r\n  background: #09191bec;\r\n  border: 1px solid #a68e5b;\r\n  box-shadow: 0 12px 28px #000;\r\n}\r\n.objective-dock span {\r\n  font:\r\n    700 0.58rem Arial,\r\n    sans-serif;\r\n  text-transform: uppercase;\r\n  letter-spacing: 0.12em;\r\n  color: var(--brass);\r\n}\r\n.objective-dock strong {\r\n  font-size: 0.9rem;\r\n}\r\n.objective-dock button {\r\n  border: 0;\r\n  background: transparent;\r\n  color: #cdb876;\r\n  text-decoration: underline;\r\n}\r\n.journal-opening {\r\n  position: fixed;\r\n  z-index: 60;\r\n  inset: 0;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  place-items: center;\r\n  background: radial-gradient(circle, #17403edb, #03090af7 70%);\r\n  backdrop-filter: blur(12px);\r\n}\r\n.wax-open {\r\n  border: 0;\r\n  background: transparent;\r\n  color: #eee2c5;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  place-items: center;\r\n  gap: 0.6rem;\r\n}\r\n.wax-open span {\r\n  width: 120px;\r\n  height: 120px;\r\n  border-radius: 50%;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  place-items: center;\r\n  font-size: 3rem;\r\n  background: radial-gradient(circle at 35% 25%, #b95a5a, #6c2028 65%, #3d1016);\r\n  border: 6px double #52161c;\r\n  box-shadow: 0 20px 30px #000;\r\n}\r\n.wax-open strong {\r\n  font-size: 1.7rem;\r\n  font-weight: 400;\r\n}\r\n.wax-open small {\r\n  color: #a6aaa0;\r\n}\r\n.ceremony-controls {\r\n  position: fixed;\r\n  z-index: 70;\r\n  top: 5.5rem;\r\n  left: 50%;\r\n  transform: #000;\r\n}\r\n.ceremony-controls span {\r\n  font-size: 0.8rem;\r\n  color: #d6c69c;\r\n}\r\n.ceremony-controls button {\r\n  background: transparent;\r\n  color: #fff;\r\n  border: 1px solid #816f4b;\r\n  padding: 0.45rem;\r\n}\r\n.replay-control {\r\n  position: fixed;\r\n  z-index: 31;\r\n  right: 1rem;\r\n  bottom: 1rem;\r\n}\r\n.stage-omen {\r\n  background: #020708;\r\n}\r\n.stage-omen .panel {\r\n  opacity: 0.5;\r\n}\r\n.stage-attention .panel,\r\n.stage-seal .panel,\r\n.stage-parchment .panel {\r\n  opacity: 0.25;\r\n  transform: scale(0.97);\r\n}\r\n.stage-attention .journal-panel,\r\n.stage-seal .journal-panel,\r\n.stage-parchment .journal-panel {\r\n  transform: scale(1.015);\r\n}\r\n.stage-seal .seal-cracks {\r\n  opacity: 1;\r\n  transition: opacity 0.7s;\r\n}\r\n.stage-seal .wax-seal {\r\n  transform: scale(1.08) rotate(-3deg);\r\n  filter: brightness(1.3);\r\n}\r\n.stage-parchment .wax-seal {\r\n  transform: scale(0.55) rotate(28deg) translate(70px, 60px);\r\n  opacity: 0.1;\r\n}\r\n.stage-parchment .right-page {\r\n  animation: page-release 0.8s var(--ease-ceremony);\r\n}\r\n.stage-map .fog-bank,\r\n.stage-active .fog-bank {\r\n  opacity: 0.15;\r\n}\r\n@keyframes page-release {\r\n  0% {\r\n    transform: perspective(1000px) rotateY(-8deg) translateX(-18px);\r\n  }\r\n  100% {\r\n    transform: perspective(1000px) rotateY(-2deg);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n.mobile-nav {\r\n  display: none;\r\n}\r\n\r\n.volume-control {\r\n  display: flex;\r\n  align-items: center;\r\n  gap: 0.35rem;\r\n  color: #aeb8ad;\r\n  font:\r\n    600 0.58rem Arial,\r\n    sans-serif;\r\n  text-transform: uppercase;\r\n  letter-spacing: 0.08em;\r\n}\r\n.volume-control input {\r\n  width: 72px;\r\n 0;\r\n}\r\n.signal-live {\r\n  color: #8fd1ba;\r\n}\r\n.mini-page {\r\n  min-height: 170px;\r\n  padding: 1.4rem;\r\n  background: #d8c69b;\r\n  color: #28251e;\r\n  box-shadow: inset 0 0 35px #72522b44;\r\n  display: grid;\r\n  align-content: center;\r\n}\r\n.mini-page span {\r\n  font:\r\n    0.6rem Arial,\r\n    sans-serif;\r\n  text-transform: uppercase;\r\n}\r\n.mini-page strong {\r\n {\r\n  max-width: 540px;\r\n  background: #d8c498;\r\n  color: #29251c;\r\n  padding: 2rem;\r\n  box-shadow: var(--shadow-deep);\r\n  border: 1px solid #ead79d;\r\n}\r\n.confirm-sheet h2 {\r\n place-items: center;\r\n}\r\n.quartermaster-login section {\r\n  color: #241f18;\r\n}\r\n.quartermaster-login input {\r\n  display: block;\r\n}\r\n.quartermaster-login .wax-emblem {\r\n  display: none;\r\n}\r\n\r\n@media (max-width: 1100px) {\r\n  .workspace {\r\n    grid-template-columns: 1fr;\r\n    display: block;\r\n    width: min(760px, 96vw);\r\n    margin-top: 1rem;\r\n  }\r\n  .workspace > * {\r\n    display: none !important;\r\n  }\r\n  .workspace > .mobile-current {\r\n    display: grid !important;\r\n  }\r\n  .journal-panel.mobile-current {\r\n    grid-template-columns: 0.72fr 1.28fr;\r\n  }\r\n  .panel {\r\n    min-height: calc(100svh - 180px);\r\n  }\r\n  .chart {\r\n    min-height: 55svh;\r\n  }\r\n  .mobile-nav {\r\n    position: fixed;\r\n    z-index: 35;\r\n    display: grid;\r\n    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);\r\n    left: 0;\r\n    right: 0;\r\n    bottom: 0;\r\n    padding: 0 max(1rem, env(safe-area-inset-right)) env(safe-area-inset-bottom) max(1rem, env(safe-area-inset-left));\r\n    background: #061416;\r\n    border-top: 1px solid #8e794b;\r\n  }\r\n  .mobile-nav button {\r\n    min-height: 3.3rem;\r\n    color: #aaa;\r\n    background: transparent;\r\n    border: 0;\r\n  }\r\n  .mobile-nav button.active {\r\n    color: #f1d98d;\r\n    border-top: 2px solid var(--brass);\r\n  }\r\n  .objective-dock {\r\n    bottom: font-size: 1.2rem;\r\n  }\r\n  .connection {\r\n    font-size: 0;\r\n  }\r\n  .connection:before {\r\n    width: 9px;\r\n    height: 9px;\r\n  }\r\n  .workspace {\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n    margin: 0 display: none;\r\n  }\r\n  .page.right-page {\r\n    border-radius: 0;\r\n    min-height: calc(100svh - 150px);\r\n    padding: 2.2rem 1.5rem calc(7rem + env(safe-area-inset-bottom));\r\n    transform: none;\r\n    clip-path: polygon(0 1%, 4% 0, 100% 1%, 99% 98%, 96% 100%, 2% 99%);\r\n  }\r\n  .sealed-content h2 {\r\n    font-size: 2.8rem;\r\n  }\r\n  .panel {\r\n    border-width: 0;\r\n {\r\n    font-size: 0.8rem;\r\n    left: 0.7rem;\r\n    right: 0.7rem;\r\n    width: auto;\r\n    transform: none;\r\n    padding: 0.65rem;\r\n  }\r\n  .objective-dock button {\r\n    min-height: 2.5rem;\r\n  }\r\n  .ceremony-controls {\r\n    top: auto;\r\n    bottom: calc(8rem + env(safe-area-inset-bottom));\r\n    width: calc(100vw - 1.4rem);\r\n    justify-content: space-between;\r\n (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {\r\n  *,\r\n  *:before,\r\n  *:after {\r\n    scroll-behavior: auto !important;\r\n    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;\r\n    animation-iteration-count: 1 ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-16T19:58:46.718Z

The project already has a solid transactional progression model and a first chapter-release ceremony, so I’m extending that foundation instead of replacing it. The main gaps are a reusable named-sequence controller, a true first-arrival/re-entry journey, physical Quartermaster authentication and action ceremonies, and broader event-specific player reveals.

string; sequence: number }>(existing: T[], incoming: T[]) {\n  const byId = new Map(existing.map((event) => [event.id, event]));\n  for (const event of incoming) byId.set(event.id, event);\n  return [...byId.values()].sort((a, b) => a.sequence - b.sequence);\n}\n\r\n\n---CEREMONY TEST---\r\nimport { describe, expect, it } from \"vitest\";\nimport { ceremonyStages, getCeremonyStages } from Math.min(stage.duration, 120);\n+  if (mode === \"gentle\") return stage.gentle ?? Math.min(stage.duration, 420);\n+  return stage.duration;\n+}\n+\n+export function useCinematicTransition(mode: MotionMode) {\n+  const [name, setName] = useState(\"dormant\");\n+  const [stage, setStage] = useState(\"settled\");\n+  const [isPlaying, setPlaying] = useState(false);\n+  const controller = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n+  const running = useRef(false);\n+\n+  const cancel = useCallback(() => controller.current?.abort(), []);\n+  const skip = useCallback(() => controller.current?.abort(), []);\n+  const runStages = useCallback(async (stages: readonly TransitionStage[], signal: AbortSignal) => {\n+    for (const next of stages) {\n+      if (signal.aborted) break;\n+      setStage(next.name);\n+      await wait(durationFor(next, mode), signal);\n+    }\n+  }, [mode]);\n+\n+  const play = useCallback(async <T,>(sequenceName: string, plan: TransitionPlan, operation?: () => Promise<T>) => {\n+    if (running.current) throw new Error(\"A cinematic transition is already underway.\");\n+    running.current = true;\n+    setPlaying(true);\n+    setName(sequenceName);\n+    const abort = new AbortController();\n+    controller.current = abort;\n+    try {\n+      await runStages(plan.opening, abort.signal);\n+      if (abort.signal.aborted) return undefined;\n+      let result: T | undefined;\n+      try {\n+        result = operation ? await operation() : undefined;\n+      } catch (error) {\n+        await runStages(plan.failure ?? }\n+\n+@keyframes ocean-breathe { to { transform:translate3d(2%,-1%,0) scale(1.04); opacity:.62; } }\n+@keyframes lantern-drift { to { transform:translate3d(5%,-3%,0) rotate(2deg); } }\n+@keyframes sea-light { 0%,20% { transform:translateX(-90%); } 70%,100% { transform:translateX(90%); } }\n+@keyframes fog-cross { to { transform:translate3d(11%,-3%,0) scale(1.08); } }\n+@keyframes introduction-depart { to { opacity:0; } }\n+@keyframes relic-weight { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(-45px) rotate(-18deg); } 70% { transform:translateY(3px) rotate(25deg); } }\n+@keyframes lock-reject { 35% { transform:translateX(-4px) rotate(-.6deg); filter:brightness(.65); } 65% { transform:translateX(3px) rotate(.4deg); } }\n+@keyframes chart-rule { from { transform:scaleX(0); } }\n+@keyframes ledger-write { from { opacity:0; clip-path:inset(0 100% 0 0); transform:translateY(-8px); } }\n+@keyframes seal-pressure { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(-40px) scale(1.18) rotate(-8deg); } 65% { opacity:1; transform:translateY(2px) scale(.94) rotate(2deg); } }\n+@keyframes lantern-sweep { to { transform:rotate(45deg); } }\n+\n+@media (max-width: 650px) {\n+  .intro-title span { font-size:clamp(2.7rem,16vw,5rem); }\n+  .intro-wave { height:58%; left:-35%; width:170%; }\n+  .intro-replay-control { bottom:calc(7.8rem + env(safe-area-inset-bottom)); }\n+  .quartermaster-login { padding:1rem; }\n+  .quartermaster-login .login-ledger { padding:2rem 1.35rem; }\n+  .gm-grid { gap:1.25rem; }\n+  .quartermaster-shell { padding:1rem max(1rem,env(safe-area-inset-right)) 2rem max(1rem,env(safe-area-inset-left)); }\n+}\n+\n+@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {\n+  .voyage-shell::before,.quartermaster-shell::before,.ocean-depth,.panel::before,.intro-fog,.mini-page::after { animation:none !important; }\n+  .intro-wave { transform:translateY(12%) !important; }\n+  .intro-title,.intro-title span { opacity:1 !important; transform:none !important; clip-path:inset(0) !important; }\n+}\n*** End Patch";

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-16T20:01:41.242Z

The shared sequence controller and the main visual layers are now in place. Backend requests begin immediately when a ceremony starts; successful visuals continue only after the real response, failures run a reversal treatment, and player state is refreshed only after its matching reveal has finished. I’m checking type safety and tests now, then I’ll correct any regressions and capture the result.

Documents\\treasurehunt\\forever-treasure-companion\\docs\\theatrical-event-system.md\n@@\n `useCeremony` owns one sorted queue and AbortController; components contain no independent timeout chains. Default timing totals about 6.3 seconds. Reduced motion uses attention, grouped ink stages, map, and active state with 120ms caps. Skip aborts presentation but still reconciles the authoritative snapshot. Replay creates a local synthetic event ID and never mutates progression. Audio is initialized only by â€œOpen the journalâ€; the seal tone is procedural and low-volume.\n+\n+## Shared cinematic controller\n+\n+`useCinematicTransition` is the shared cancellable sequence runner for arrival, authentication, and progression presentation. A plan has named opening, success, and failure stages. For server-backed commands the real request starts immediately alongside the opening stage. The success branch cannot begin until that request succeeds; a rejected request runs the plan's physical reversal and never reveals authoritative state. Abort on unmount prevents orphaned stage timers, duplicate starts are rejected, and CSS ambience pauses while the document is hidden.\n+\n+Major full-motion durations are approximately: first arrival 7.95s, same-session re-entry 1.55s, sign-in success 4.72s, prepare 2.5s, release 4.4s in the Quartermaster plus the existing 6.25s player reveal, solve 2.55s, relic award 3.6s, map reveal 4.15s, pause 2.55s, resume 2.55s, and undo 2.7s. Gentle motion keeps the order with 280â€“420ms stages. Reduced motion keeps the narrative ordering with stages capped at 120ms.\n+\n+The browser session key `forever-intro:<campaign>` selects the long first arrival once per session. The always-available **Replay introduction** control forces it locally and never changes story state. Development builds also expose an **Animation lab** containing every named sequence; production builds omit it.\n+\n+All added material effects are original procedural CSS. No third-party visual or audio assets were added.\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

Audio is initialized only by â€œOpen the journalâ€; the seal tone is procedural and low-volume.\n+\n+## Shared cinematic controller\n+\n+`useCinematicTransition` is the shared cancellable sequence runner for arrival, authentication, and progression presentation. A plan has named opening, success, and failure stages. For server-backed commands the real request starts immediately alongside the opening stage. The success branch cannot begin until that request succeeds; a rejected request runs the plan's physical reversal and never reveals authoritative state. Abort on unmount prevents orphaned stage timers, duplicate starts are rejected, and CSS ambience pauses while the document is hidden.\n+\n+Major full-motion durations are approximately: first arrival 7.95s, same-session re-entry 1.55s, sign-in success 4.72s, prepare 2.5s, release 4.4s in the Quartermaster plus the existing 6.25s player reveal, solve 2.55s, relic award 3.6s, map reveal 4.15s, pause 2.55s, resume 2.55s, and undo 2.7s. Gentle motion keeps the order with 280â€“420ms stages. Reduced motion keeps the narrative ordering with stages capped at 120ms.\n+\n+The browser session key `forever-intro:<campaign>` selects the long first arrival once per session. The always-available **Replay introduction** control forces it locally and never changes story state. Development builds also expose an **Animation lab** containing every named sequence; production builds omit it.\n+\n+All added material effects are original procedural CSS. No third-party visual or audio assets were added.\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

relic frame.\"],\r\n  [\"REVEAL_MAP\", \"Reveal Test Map Location\", \"Mark Port Merrick on the voyage chart.\"],\r\n  [\"REVEAL_ROUTE\", \"Reveal Route Segment\", \"Draw the next safe development route between revealed locations.\"],\r\n  [\r\n    \"REVEAL_ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE\",\r\n    \"Reveal Artifact Silhouette\",\r\n    \"Expose only the next artifactâ€™s approved safe outline.\",\r\n  ],\r\n  [\r\n    \"CONNECT_ARTIFACTS\",\r\n    \"Connect Test Artifacts\",\r\n    \"Reveal the neutral development connection between configured relics.\",\r\n  ],\r\n  [\"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\", \"Discover Side Quest\", \"Move the next optional mystery from rumor to discovered.\"],\r\n  [\"UPDATE_SIDE_QUEST\", \"Update Side Quest\", \"Advance one released optional objective.\"],\r\n  [\"COMPLETE_SIDE_QUEST\", \"Complete Side Quest\", \"Complete the active optional mystery and grant its safe reward.\"],\r\n  [\"ADD_JOURNAL_ANNOTATION\", \"Add Journal Annotation\", \"Release a generic development note beside the active chapter.\"],\r\n  [\"ADD_LOG_ENTRY\", \"Add Player Log Entry\", \"Record a generic player-facing captainâ€™s note.\"],\r\n  [\"TEASE_FINALE\", \"Tease Sealed Finale\", \"Wake the dormant shell without releasing finale content.\"],\r\n  [\"UPDATE_FINALE_REQUIREMENT\", \"Update Finale Requirement\", \"Advance one generic symbolic requirement.\"],\r\n  [\r\n    \"UNDO_LAST\",\r\n    \"Undo Last Progression Action\",\r\n    \"Restore the last saved progression state and publish a reconciliation event.\",\r\n  ],\r\n  [\"PAUSE\", \"Pause Campaign\", \"Pause the voyage without hiding already released material.\"],\r\n  [\"RESUME\", \"Resume Campaign\", \"Return the voyage to active status.\"],\r\n] as const;\r\nexport function Quartermaster({ authenticated }: { authenticated: boolean }) {\r\n  const [signedIn, setSignedIn] = useState(authenticated);\r\n  const [status, setStatus] = useState<Status | null>(null);\r\n  const [selected, setSelected] = useState<(typeof actions)[number] | null>(null);\r\n  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);\r\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\r\n  const [motionMode, setMotionMode] = useState<MotionMode>(\"full\");\r\n  const transition = useCinematicTransition(motionMode);\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const media = matchMedia(\"(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)\");\r\n    const sync = () =>\r\n      setMotionMode(\r\n        media.matches || localStorage.getItem(\"forever-motion\") === \"reduced\"\r\n          ? \"reduced\"\r\n          : localStorage.getItem(\"forever-motion\") === \"gentle\"\r\n            ? \"gentle\"\r\n            : \"full\",\r\n      );\r\n    queueMicrotask(sync);\r\n    media.addEventListener(\"change\", sync);\r\n    return () => media.removeEventListener(\"change\", sync);\r\n  }, []);\r\n  const refresh = useCallback(async () => {\r\n    const response = await fetch(\"/api/gm/status\", { cache: \"no-store\" });\r\n    if (response.ok) {\r\n      setStatus(await response.json());\r\n      setSignedIn(true);\r\n    }\r\n  }, []);\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (!signedIn) return;\r\n    void fetch(\"/api/gm/status\", { cache: \"no-store\" }).then(async (response) => {\r\n      if (response.ok) setStatus(await response.json());\r\n    });\r\n  }, [signedIn]);\r\n  async function login(event: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) {\r\n    event.preventDefault();\r\n    setError(\"\");\r\n    const form = new FormData(event.currentTarget);\r\n    try {\r\n      await transition.play(\"signIn\", cinematicSequences.signIn, async () => {\r\n        const response = await fetch(\"/api/gm/login\", {\r\n          method: \"POST\",\r\n          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n          body: JSON.stringify({ username: form.get(\"username\"), [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT]"password\") }),\r\n        });\r\n        const data = await response.json();\r\n        if (!response.ok) throw new Error(data.error);\r\n        return data;\r\n      });\r\n      setSignedIn(true);\r\n      void refresh();\r\n    } catch (cause) {\r\n      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"The lock refused the key.\");\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  async function execute() {\r\n    if (!selected || !status) return;\r\n    setBusy(true);\r\n    setError(\"\");\r\n    const action = selected;\r\n    const sequenceByAction: Record<string, CinematicSequenceName> = {\r\n      PREPARE_CHAPTER: \"prepare\",\r\n      RELEASE_CHAPTER: \"release\",\r\n      MARK_SOLVED: \"solved\",\r\n      AWARD_ARTIFACT: \"artifact\",\r\n      REVEAL_MAP: \"map\",\r\n      REVEAL_ROUTE: \"map\",\r\n \"undo\",\r\n      PAUSE: \"pause\",\r\n      RESUME: \"resume\",\r\n    };\r\n    try {\r\n      const data = await transition.play(\r\n        sequenceByAction[action[0]],\r\n ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-16T20:28:14.982Z

The first full validation pass passed formatting, lint, type checking, all 13 unit tests, database verification, and the cross-browser access checks. One browser test exposed a real choreography/testing issue: the parchment and ink stages were too brief to be reliably perceived and captured. I’m lengthening those two major beats while keeping the total release ceremony under eight seconds, then I’ll rerun the full journey.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-16T20:35:58.056Z

The next failure was in the test harness rather than the experience: the Quartermaster’s response can legitimately arrive after the player’s ordered event, so the test was waiting on the wrong side of that race. I’ve changed the capture to observe the player ceremony immediately after confirmation while separately verifying the command dialog closes, which matches the real event architecture.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-16T20:56:09.580Z

Implemented the cinematic UI and journey-animation pass while preserving real progression, authentication, persistence, SSE synchronization, CSRF protection, audit history, and undo behavior.

### What changed

- Added a shared cancellable cinematic sequence controller with named stages, duplicate prevention, unmount cleanup, backend success/failure branches, and hidden-tab animation pausing.
- Added full first-arrival and shorter same-session re-entry journeys.
- Reworked Quartermaster authentication around a cabin door, ledger, brass latch, lantern sweep, success ceremony, and physical rejection state.
- Added purpose-built ceremonies for prepare, release, solve, artifact, map, pause, resume, undo, side quests, annotations, routes, and finale preparation.
- Significantly improved parchment, page curvature, book gutter, wax, brass, velvet, wood, fog, water lighting, objective dock, map, relic altar, ledger, and Quartermaster surfaces.
- Added Full, Gentle, and Reduced Motion modes with persistence and `prefers-reduced-motion` support.
- Added development-only Animation Lab controls; production excludes them.
- Updated responsive compositions and tests for current development-safe story content.

### Major durations

- First arrival: ~7.95s
- Same-session re-entry: ~1.55s
- Authentication success: ~4.72s
- Prepare: ~2.5s
- Quartermaster release: ~4.4s
- Player chapter release: ~7.75s
- Solve: ~2.55s
- Relic placement: ~3.6s
- Map reveal: ~4.15s
- Pause/resume: ~2.55s each
- Undo: ~2.7s

Gentle Motion preserves sequencing with restrained 280–420ms stages. Reduced Motion caps stages around 120ms.

### Validation

Final validation passed:

- Formatting and lint
- TypeScript
- 13/13 unit tests
- 5 browser tests passed, 1 intentionally skipped
- Complete Chromium progression journey
- Chromium and mobile WebKit access gates
- Database verification: 8 events, 8 audit entries
- Production build
- Production restart safety
- Responsive captures at 2560×1440, 1920×1080, 1440×900, 430×932, 390×844, and 844×390

[Open all validation screenshots](</C:/Users/kgray/AppData/Local/ForeverTreasureCompanion/validation/artifacts/validation>)

![Active voyage screen](C:/Users/kgray/AppData/Local/ForeverTreasureCompanion/validation/artifacts/validation/07-active-chapter.png)

### Key implementation files

Added:

- [useCinematicTransition.ts](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/src/components/cinematic/useCinematicTransition.ts>)
- [sequences.ts](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/src/components/cinematic/sequences.ts>)
- [sequences.test.ts](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/src/components/cinematic/sequences.test.ts>)
- [cinematic-system.md](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/docs/cinematic-system.md>)

Principal modified files:

- [PlayerExperience.tsx](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx>)
- [Quartermaster.tsx](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/src/components/gm/Quartermaster.tsx>)
- [globals.css](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/src/app/globals.css>)
- [acceptance.spec.ts](</\\gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/treasurehunt/forever-treasure-companion/tests/e2e/acceptance.spec.ts>)

No external visual or audio assets were added; the new material and environmental treatments are original procedural CSS. Sound remains permission-gated and respects mute and master volume.

### Replaying animations

- Player introduction: select **Replay introduction** at the lower left.
- Chapter ceremony: select **Replay ceremony** after a release.
- Force a new-session introduction: remove `forever-intro:development-forever-treasure` from session storage or begin a new browser session.
- Development: open **Animation Lab** on either interface to run every named sequence without mutating progression.
- Authentication journey: clear the Quartermaster session and revisit `/quartermaster`.

No fake progression state, fake loading delays, or artificial backend pauses were introduced. Success presentation only follows a real successful save; failures use their reversal sequence.
